message:
🌱 Add k0s: k0s should move /var/lib/k0s/kubelet to /var/lib/kubelet mission (#810)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "k0s-1842-k0s-should-move-var-lib-k0s-kubelet-to-var-lib-kubelet",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "k0s: k0s should move /var/lib/k0s/kubelet to /var/lib/kubelet",
+    "description": "k0s should move /var/lib/k0s/kubelet to /var/lib/kubelet. Community-reported issue.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify k0s troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your k0s deployment:\n```bash\nk0s version\nk0s status\njournalctl -u k0scontroller --no-pager --lines=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review k0s configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant k0s configuration:\n```bash\nk0s version\nk0s kubectl get nodes -o wide\nk0s kubectl get pods -A\n```\n### Before creating an issue, make sure you've checked the following:\n\n- [X] You are running the latest released version of k0s\n- [X] Make sure you've searched for existing issues, both open and closed\n- [X] Make sure you've searched for PRs too, a"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for k0s should move /var/lib/k0s/kubelet to /var/lib/kubelet",
+        "description": "It's a bug of aws-ebs-csi-driver helm chart. I've submitted a fix: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/aws-ebs-csi-driver/pull/1276\n\nAs a workaround you can try to add the following to the values:\n```\nsidecars:\n  nodeDriverRegistrar:\n    env: \n      - name: DRIVER_REG_SOCK_PATH\n        value:\n```yaml\nNo LSB modules are available.\n\nDistributor ID:\tUbuntu\nDescription:\tUbuntu 22.04 LTS\nRelease:\t22.04\nCodename:\tjammy\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm k0s should move /var/lib/k0s/kubelet to\u2026 is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\njournalctl -u k0scontroller --no-pager --lines=50 --since='5 minutes ago'\nk0s kubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "It's a bug of aws-ebs-csi-driver helm chart. I've submitted a fix: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/aws-ebs-csi-driver/pull/1276\n\nAs a workaround you can try to add the following to the values:\n```\nsidecars:\n  nodeDriverRegistrar:\n    env: \n      - name: DRIVER_REG_SOCK_PATH\n        value: /var/lib/k0s/kubelet/plugins/ebs.csi.aws.com/csi.sock\n```",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "No LSB modules are available.\n\nDistributor ID:\tUbuntu\nDescription:\tUbuntu 22.04 LTS\nRelease:\t22.04\nCodename:\tjammy",
+        "sidecars:\n  nodeDriverRegistrar:\n    env: \n      - name: DRIVER_REG_SOCK_PATH\n        value: /var/lib/k0s/kubelet/plugins/ebs.csi.aws.com/csi.sock",
+        "sidecars:\n  nodeDriverRegistrar:\n    env:\n      - name: DRIVER_REG_SOCK_PATH\n        value: /var/lib/k0s/kubelet/plugins/ebs.csi.aws.com/csi.sock\n\nnode:\n  kubeletPath: /var/lib/k0s/kubelet"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "k0s",
+      "sandbox",
+      "orchestration",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "k0s"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [
+      "Pod",
+      "Storageclass"
+    ],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "sandbox",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/k0sproject/k0s/issues/1842",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/k0sproject/k0s"
+    },
+    "reactions": 4,
+    "comments": 26,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "k0s",
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running k0s cluster or a system where k0s can be installed."
+  },
